sourceMovie.title: Due Date
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Due Date (2010) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Todd Phillips's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Little Miss Sunshine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Due Date clicked for you, Little Miss Sunshine (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Due Date?
faq.answer: Try Little Miss Sunshine, The Apartment, Interstate 60—each shares DNA with Due Date while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Due Date?
faq.answer: Due Date (2010) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Due Date worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
